input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI targeting Android + Windows on Windows 11:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest SDK version
Invoke-RestMethod https://dotnetcli.azureedge.net/dotnet/Sdk/LTS/latest.version

# Install via winget (recommended)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8  # or .9 depending on latest LTS
```

## 2. Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to find exact names
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (pulls in android, ios, maccatalyst dependencies)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Java JDK (required for Android)

```powershell
# MAUI/Android needs JDK 17
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Verify
java --version
```

## 4. Install Android SDK components

The MAUI workload auto-installs some Android bits, but you can manage them explicitly:

```powershell
# Find where Android SDK was placed
dotnet android sdk info

# Or locate it manually (default path)
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
# Alternative MAUI-managed path:
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"

# List installed/available packages
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install specific API level + build tools (discover latest dynamically)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-34" `
  "build-tools;34.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Create an Android emulator

```powershell
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  --name "Pixel_API_34" `
  --package "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_6"
```

## 6. Enable Windows App SDK target (for WinUI3)

```powershell
# Ensure Windows App SDK is available (comes with MAUI workload)
# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## 7. Set environment variables

```powershell
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17", "User")
```

## 8. Verify the full toolchain

```powershell
dotnet --info
dotnet workload list
dotnet new maui --list   # confirm templates available
```

## 9. Create and run a test project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Run on Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

---

**Key tips:**
- Use `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current.
- The target framework monikers (TFMs) like `net8.0-android` change with SDK version — check your `.csproj` after project creation.
- If `winget` isn't available, use the [dotnet-install script](https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.ps1) instead:
  ```powershell
  Invoke-WebRequest 'https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.ps1' -OutFile dotnet-install.ps1
  ./dotnet-install.ps1 -Channel LTS
  ```
